summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's compositing and layers area have introduced various enhancements aimed at improving performance and responsiveness during user interactions. Notably, **these changes enhance touch response for web pages, particularly through refinements to the Async Pan/Zoom (APZ) framework**, which helps manage input for smoother scrolling and panning experiences. The updates streamline how touch events are processed, leading to quicker responses during actions like fast flinging and touch movements while also better managing the rendering of high dynamic range (HDR) content on systems utilizing the Wayland display server. Overall, these improvements contribute to more efficient rendering and a smoother experience for users navigating web content.
